tasks.summary: Create api_keys database table and model
tasks.description: Migration for api_keys table (id, user_id, key_hash, prefix, label, revoked_at, created_at). Model with create/verify/revoke methods. Key generation uses crypto.randomBytes.

acceptance.criterion: Migration creates api_keys table with required columns
ApiKey model exports create, verify, and revoke methods
acceptance.verify: grep -q 'export.*create' src/models/api-key.ts && grep -q 'export.*verify' src/models/api-key.ts && grep -q 'export.*revoke' src/models/api-key.ts

acceptance.criterion: Unit tests cover key creation, verification, and revocation

tasks.description: New strategy that extracts API key from Authorization: Bearer sk_live_... header, looks up by prefix, verifies hash, and attaches user to request context. Register in middleware chain after JwtStrategy.

acceptance.criterion: ApiKeyStrategy file exists and exports the strategy

tasks.description: POST /api/keys (create, returns raw key once), GET /api/keys (list user's keys, no hashes), DELETE /api/keys/:id (soft revoke). All require JWT auth — you manage keys via your session, use keys for programmatic access.

acceptance.criterion: POST /api/keys returns 201 with key prefix and raw key
All endpoints return 401 without valid JWT

tasks.summary: Integration test: full API key lifecycle
tasks.description: End-to-end test: create user, login, create API key, use API key to access protected endpoint, revoke key, verify access denied.

acceptance.criterion: Integration test covers create, use, revoke, deny lifecycle
